title: Implement rr Native Messaging host runtime entrypoint
description: Wire the shipped rr binary to act as the Native Messaging host at runtime. The current manifest points at rr, but packages/cli/src/main.rs only runs normal CLI parsing and never reads Native Messaging stdin/stdout envelopes, so browser launch requests hang. This slice should add an explicit Roger-owned host mode or equivalent entry path that reads BridgeLaunchIntent, calls roger_bridge::handle_bridge_intent, and writes BridgeResponse back to stdout.
acceptance_criteria:
The rr binary has a real runtime path for Native Messaging host execution rather than only install-time manifest wiring
When launched as the Native Messaging host, rr reads a BridgeLaunchIntent from stdin, routes it through roger_bridge::handle_bridge_intent, and writes a BridgeResponse to stdout
Browser Start/Resume/Findings/Refresh no longer hang at dispatch when host registration is otherwise healthy
Validation includes a direct host-process smoke plus browser-side launch smoke with exact commands and observed response envelopes
notes: Primary files: packages/cli/src/main.rs and/or packages/cli/src/lib.rs runtime entrypoint, plus bridge/CLI smoke tests. This bead closes only on actual host-process execution evidence, not manifest installation or doctor checks alone.
close_reason: Implemented rr Native Messaging runtime host path in packages/cli/src/main.rs (stdin envelope -> roger_bridge::handle_bridge_intent -> stdout envelope). Added host-process envelope smoke coverage in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs and stabilized extension setup smoke lock contention. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke (pass), rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge --test bridge_smoke (pass), rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (pass), rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (pass).

title: Define Roger CalVer tags, channels, and version-derivation contract
description: Freeze the concrete CalVer scheme Roger will use for GitHub releases and artifact names, including stable vs rc/pre-release channels, tag format, rerun/build metadata rules, Cargo workspace version interaction, and how GitHub Actions derives the canonical release version without hand edits. This bead exists because the old release beads stopped at artifact ownership and install semantics; they never answered the operational versioning question, so there is no machine-truthful source for tags, release names, archive names, or self-update lookup. Acceptance: (1) one canonical CalVer format is documented and implemented in a small Roger-owned derivation path; (2) stable/rc/nightly semantics and provenance rules are explicit; (3) artifact names and release metadata fields derive from the same source; (4) the contract states why prior rr-001.2/rr-001.3 closure was insufficient: ownership was defined, but version authority was not. Validation contract: int layer via a deterministic version-derivation test command and a CI dry-run on synthetic refs.
close_reason: Implemented CalVer contract + derivation path: added docs/RELEASE_CALVER_VERSIONING_CONTRACT.md, scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py, scripts/release/test_derive_calver_version.sh, and .github/workflows/release-version-dry-run.yml. Validation (int): ./scripts/release/test_derive_calver_version.sh; python3 scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py --ref refs/tags/v2026.03.31 --sha 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef01234567 --today 2026.04.01 --workspace-version 0.1.0 | jq -e '.channel=="stable" and .version=="2026.03.31" and .tag=="v2026.03.31"' >/dev/null && python3 scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py --ref refs/tags/v2026.03.31-rc.2 --sha 89abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef --today 2026.04.01 --workspace-version 0.1.0 | jq -e '.channel=="rc" and .version=="2026.03.31-rc.2" and .tag=="v2026.03.31-rc.2"' >/dev/null && python3 scripts/release/derive_calver_version.py --ref refs/heads/main --sha fedcba9876543210fedcba9876543210fedcba98 --today 2026.04.01 --workspace-version 0.1.0 | jq -e '.channel=="nightly" and .version=="2026.04.01-nightly.fedcba987654" and .tag=="nightly-2026.04.01-fedcba987654"' >/dev/null

title: Fix repo/PR rr status resolution for live sessions
description:

3. RR_STORE_ROOT=/tmp/rrprobe1 rr status --repo owner/repo --pr 11 --robot -> incorrectly returns empty/no matching repo-local session found
4. RR_STORE_ROOT=/tmp/rrprobe1 rr status --session <id> --robot -> works

title: Harden swarm bead operability and queue trust
description: Cass analysis of recent Roger Reviewer swarm sessions shows recurring efficiency loss around bead pickup, queue trust, and control-plane overhead. This epic owns the narrow operability remediation lane so the swarm can spend less time rediscovering queue state and more time delivering implementation work.
acceptance_criteria:
Work is split into explicit leaves covering queue-health preflight, prompt-cost reduction, pre-launch bead-batch hygiene, maintenance-lane separation, and bounded validation
The remediation stays focused on swarm/bead operability rather than changing Roger product scope
The resulting graph gives operators a concrete path to improve bead pickup efficiency before the next large swarm run
notes:
Planning source: docs/SWARM_BEAD_OPERABILITY_REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
Cass evidence behind this lane: repeated br ready distrust, lock-retry instructions baked into swarm scripts, queue-empty ambiguity, and repeated mid-run discovery of missing or unhealthy beads.
close_reason: Epic closeout after child remediation lane completed: rr-qoq.1 preflight guard, rr-qoq.2 short-form worker prompt split, rr-qoq.3 bead-batch audit, rr-qoq.4 maintenance-lane separation, rr-qoq.5 bounded rehearsal validation. Acceptance met: explicit leaf split delivered, scope remained operability-only, and concrete operator path now exists for improving queue trust before larger swarm runs.

title: Add swarm preflight for pinned br path, queue health, and launch readiness
description: Operators currently learn too late that the pinned br path is broken, queue health is noisy, or the bead workspace is not swarm-ready. This bead adds a swarm-oriented preflight path that fails loudly on those conditions before a large run starts.
acceptance_criteria:
Swarm preflight fails loudly when the default pinned br path is broken, the workspace health assumptions are violated, or the queue is not ready for a multi-agent run
Preflight output distinguishes operator-actionable failures from transient retry conditions instead of silently treating them all as queue emptiness
The documented preflight path becomes the canonical swarm-launch guard in repo-local runbooks or scripts
notes: This bead should build on rr-001.6 rather than reintroducing backup-binary names as the normal path.
close_reason: Added canonical swarm preflight path via scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh: resolves pinned br path, retries/labels transient database-busy conditions (exit 75), fails loudly on operator-actionable health/queue issues, and checks launch readiness (agent mail, tmux session, ready/open queue). Wired launch_swarm.sh to run preflight by default (with --no-preflight escape hatch) and updated check_prereqs/runbook to use preflight as canonical guard. Validation: bash -n scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh scripts/swarm/check_prereqs.sh; scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh --help; scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh --help | rg preflight; scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh --session roger-reviewer-preflight-smoke --codex 0 --claude 0 --gemini 0 --opencode 0 (observed fail-loud PREFLIGHT_CLASS=operator_actionable under current br doctor failure); scripts/swarm/check_prereqs.sh --session roger-reviewer-preflight-smoke --codex 0 --claude 0 --gemini 0 --opencode 0 (propagates classified preflight failure).

title: Compress swarm worker prompts into short-form operating instructions with authority links
description: Workers repeatedly reload a long control-plane manual before they can start useful work. This bead owns reducing the prompt tax by keeping only behavior-changing rules in the worker prompt and pushing the longer doctrine into authoritative linked docs.
acceptance_criteria:
Worker startup prompts are shortened to the rules that materially change immediate behavior, with longer doctrine moved into authoritative linked docs
The short-form prompt still preserves the non-negotiable safety and coordination rules around approval-sensitive work, bead claiming, and Agent Mail hygiene
Swarm docs clearly explain which file is the concise worker prompt versus the longer operator or doctrine reference
notes: This is about reducing repeated prompt cost, not weakening the operating rules.
close_reason: Compressed worker startup prompt path by splitting doctrine: overnight-marching-orders is now concise behavior-focused guidance with authority links; full long-form rules moved to docs/swarm/worker-operating-doctrine.md. Updated prompt injection in scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh and scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh to keep only immediate behavior-changing rules plus doctrine link, preserving safety/coordination non-negotiables. Updated docs/OVERNIGHT_SWARM_RUNBOOK.md to clearly label concise prompt vs long-form doctrine. Validation: bash -n scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh; scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md DemoAgent <tmp> implementation; confirmed generated prompt (43 lines) contains short-form loop + non-negotiables + doctrine link.

title: Add a pre-swarm bead-batch audit so missing leaves are found before launch
description: Recent swarm runs lost efficiency when workers discovered missing leaf beads, dependency mistakes, or prematurely closed work during execution. This bead adds an explicit operator workflow to audit and polish the next parallel bead batch before a large swarm is launched.
acceptance_criteria:
Repo-local swarm docs or scripts define an explicit pre-launch audit for the next parallel bead batch, including missing-leaf discovery, dependency sanity, and acceptance-clarity checks
The audit path is cheap enough to run before large swarms and is positioned as operator prep rather than worker-side rediscovery
The workflow defines what to do when useful work exists but the current graph does not yet expose it cleanly through br ready
notes: The intent is to move mid-run bead creation or repair into operator preflight whenever the next safe slice is already obvious.
close_reason: Implemented pre-swarm bead-batch audit script and operator workflow: added scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh with missing-leaf discovery, dependency-sanity checks, acceptance-clarity checks, database-busy retry handling, and queue-repair playbook for thin/empty br ready states. Updated docs/OVERNIGHT_SWARM_RUNBOOK.md and docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md to position the audit as pre-launch operator prep. Validation run: bash -n scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh; ./scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh --limit 8 (exit 0, warnings expected); ./scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh --limit 8 --strict (exit 2 on warnings).

title: Separate maintenance-lane bead repairs from implementation swarm flow
description: Meta-operability and bead-health work currently competes with implementation work in the same swarm operating loop. This bead defines and documents a clear maintenance lane so tracker repair work does not keep destabilizing normal implementation pickup.
acceptance_criteria:
Repo-local swarm guidance defines a clear maintenance lane for bead-health and operability repairs, including when those tasks should be isolated from implementation workers
Swarm runbooks or supervisor behavior stop treating tracker-repair work as an incidental background responsibility for every worker by default
The workflow still allows deliberate steering into maintenance work when that is the highest-leverage blocker for the swarm
notes: This should lower noise in the normal implementation frontier without hiding real workspace-health problems.
close_reason: Defined a dedicated maintenance lane policy in docs/swarm/maintenance-lane-policy.md and maintenance marching prompt in docs/swarm/maintenance-marching-orders.md; updated scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh and scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh so implementation workers explicitly avoid incidental tracker-repair duties by default; added lane selection to scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh via --lane/--maintenance-lane and passed lane into prompt generation. Validation run: bash -n scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh scripts/swarm/launch_swarm.sh scripts/swarm/run_agent_loop.sh; scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/maintenance-marching-orders.md BlueCat <tmp> maintenance + prompt grep; scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md BlueCat <tmp> implementation + prompt grep.

title: Validate swarm queue-trust remediation with a bounded rehearsal run
description: The swarm-operability fixes should be proven in one bounded rehearsal instead of assumed. This bead validates that the new preflight, prompt shape, and maintenance-lane workflow materially reduce startup ambiguity and queue trust problems without introducing a heavier operator loop.
acceptance_criteria:
One bounded rehearsal run or equivalent operator validation proves the new preflight, short-form prompt path, and maintenance-lane guidance are usable in practice
Validation records what ambiguity or startup cost was removed, what still remains, and whether queue trust improved enough for the next larger swarm run
The validation stays bounded and does not become an excuse to launch another large uncontrolled swarm before the lane is ready
notes: This is a real-world operability check, not a product E2E expansion.
close_reason: Completed bounded rehearsal validation and recorded results in docs/SWARM_QUEUE_TRUST_REHEARSAL_20260331.md, with runbook link update. Validation commands: scripts/swarm/preflight_swarm.sh --session roger-reviewer-rehearsal --codex 1 --claude 0 --gemini 0 --opencode 0 (exit 1, classified fail-loud: PREFLIGHT_CLASS=operator_actionable); scripts/swarm/audit_bead_batch.sh --limit 10 --strict (exit 2 with 9 warnings, 0 errors); scripts/swarm/build_prompt.sh docs/swarm/overnight-marching-orders.md DemoImpl <tmp> implementation and DemoMaint maintenance (both 43 lines, doctrine 81 lines). Verdict captured: startup ambiguity reduced and queue trust improved for bounded runs, but not yet ready for another large uncontrolled launch until br doctor health and warning density improve.

title: Tighten extension entry UX around GitHub-native injection and toolbar fallback
description: The current extension ships as a floating launch panel, which satisfies basic launch behavior but falls short of the intended first-class PR-page flow. Roger should prefer GitHub-native inline placement on PR pages and should expose a manual browser-action fallback when inline DOM attachment is unavailable or broken by GitHub DOM drift. Validation contract: this parent closes only after child slices land for inline placement, toolbar fallback, and truthful validation/manual smoke evidence.
close_reason: Parent integration closeout complete. Child beads rr-r3dt.1 (inline PR placement), rr-r3dt.2 (popup fallback), and rr-r3dt.3 (validation/smoke) are all closed with explicit evidence. Parent closeout validation in this cycle: rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (PASS), rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (PASS), scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-r3dt --local-only-reason "Parent integration bead; child slices rr-r3dt.1/.2/.3 are closed with evidence, and full workspace build/test rerun in this parent closeout cycle." (PASS).

title: Implement GitHub-native inline PR entry placement for Roger extension actions
description: Replace the free-floating overlay as the primary PR-page surface with GitHub-native inline placement. The extension should attach Roger actions to a stable PR-page seam such as the header/action region, use GitHub-like button and card styling, and reserve a floating panel only as a bounded last-resort fallback when no stable seam exists. Validation contract: content-script/unit coverage for anchor selection and idempotent reinjection plus a manual or smoke check on live GitHub PR pages showing inline placement on supported browsers.
close_reason: Implemented GitHub-native inline PR entry placement in extension content script with bounded floating fallback; added content-script unit tests for anchor priority + idempotent reinjection. Validation: node --test apps/extension/src/background.test.js apps/extension/src/content/main.test.js; live GitHub PR smoke probe returned inline anchor hit on [class*="prc-PageHeader-Actions-"]; rch exec -- cargo build --workspace; rch exec -- cargo test --workspace; scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-r3dt.1 --local-only-reason "Non-CI-sensitive extension content-script UX bead; validated via local unit tests, live GitHub PR smoke probe, and full workspace build/test in this cycle."

title: Add browser-action popup fallback for manual Roger launch from the current tab
description: Pressing the browser extension button should provide a manual fallback entrypoint that feels native to GitHub and Roger. On GitHub PR tabs it should offer the same bounded Start, Resume, Findings, and Refresh actions against the current PR context; on non-PR tabs it should explain the limitation and point the user to open a PR page or use local Roger. Validation contract: popup behavior tests for PR-context detection and action routing, plus manual smoke in Edge or another supported browser.
close_reason: Closeout evidence complete: node --test apps/extension/src/background.test.js apps/extension/src/popup/main.test.js PASS; supported-browser popup smoke PASS via /tmp harness (PR + non-PR contexts, canonical launch payload dispatch observed); rch exec -- cargo build --workspace PASS; rch exec -- cargo test --workspace PASS; check_ci_closeout_evidence local-only PASS.

title: Validate extension entry UX across inline placement and popup fallback
description: Defend the new extension-entry promises with truthful validation instead of screenshots or prose. This bead should add the cheapest honest coverage for inline DOM attachment, GitHub-style degraded fallback when attachment fails, and browser-action popup routing from the current tab. Validation contract: content-script or popup tests where practical, plus a bounded supported-browser manual or smoke script that records exact observed behavior and any remaining DOM-fragility caveats.
close_reason: Added extension entry UX validation coverage and bounded smoke runbook/script. Changes: (1) apps/extension/src/content/main.test.js adds inline-seam placement assertion; (2) apps/extension/src/popup/main.test.js adds PR-tab view-model assertion; (3) scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh adds executable smoke guard for background/content/popup tests plus required supported-browser suite-id presence check and explicit caveat output; (4) docs/extension-entry-ux-smoke.md documents scope, command, pass criteria, manual follow-on, and DOM-seam caveat. Validation this closeout cycle: scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh (PASS), rch exec -- cargo build --workspace (PASS), rch exec -- cargo test --workspace (PASS), scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-r3dt.3 --local-only-reason "Non-CI-sensitive extension UX validation bead; validated via scripts/swarm/smoke_extension_entry_ux.sh plus full workspace build/test in current closeout cycle." (PASS).

title: Hard-enforce CI-sensitive closeout evidence before bead closure
description: Gap identified by rr-4o8s audit: CI-sensitive closeout evidence is currently checklist/script-driven and still optional at the point of `br close`, so an agent can close `ci/github-actions/release/publish` beads without machine-enforced run URL + outcome proof. Implement a Roger-owned enforcement path (wrapper, guard command, or close helper integration) that fails closed for CI-sensitive beads unless remote evidence is supplied in the close invocation context. Acceptance: (1) CI-sensitive categories are enforced at close time rather than only documented; (2) closing a CI-sensitive bead without run URL + outcome is rejected with actionable guidance; (3) non-CI beads can still close with explicit local-only reasoning; (4) worker docs point to the enforced path, not only advisory guidance. Validation contract: docs/process + script-test/manual-smoke layer with one negative rehearsal (CI-sensitive close blocked without evidence) and one positive rehearsal (same bead closes when evidence is supplied).
close_reason:
Implemented hard close-time CI evidence enforcement via scripts/swarm/close_bead_with_ci_guard.sh and updated worker docs to use the enforced close path.

Validation:
- bash scripts/swarm/test_check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh (pass; includes guard negative+positive scenarios)
- scripts/swarm/close_bead_with_ci_guard.sh --bead rr-rqxy --reason "negative rehearsal" (expected fail: CI-sensitive bead requires remote run evidence)
- scripts/swarm/close_bead_with_ci_guard.sh --bead rr-rqxy --reason "positive rehearsal dry-run" --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success --dry-run (pass)
- scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-rqxy --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success (pass)
CI closeout evidence: run_url=https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195; outcome=success

title: Publish initial 0.1.0 release artifacts via CalVer GitHub Actions
description: Execute the implemented CalVer GitHub Actions release system to publish the first 0.1.0 stable release of Roger Reviewer, including the  binary and installer metadata. This will enable the  script to successfully download and install the application.
notes:
Correction (2026-04-02 UTC): prior close evidence referenced v2026.04.02 as installer-ready, but live install smoke showed missing release-core-manifest/checksum-shape mismatch for rr-install path. Applied and pushed fixes on main:
- 65fe46b fix: include core manifest in publishable release assets
- d643a1f fix: keep release asset fixture test portable on runners
- efd159c fix: allow installer fallback to SHA256SUMS
- d515aa8 fix: stable channel tag resolution + checksum fallback robustness
Published corrected stable release:
- release-publish success run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195
- release URL: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/releases/tag/v2026.04.03
- release assets now include release-core-manifest-2026.04.03.json and release-install-metadata-2026.04.03.json.
Validation executed:
- bash scripts/release/test_release_publish_workflow.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_validate_upstream_run.sh
- bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh
- live smoke: bash scripts/release/rr-install.sh --version 2026.04.03 --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --install-dir <tmp> and <tmp>/rr robot-docs commands --robot
- stable-channel dry-run now resolves v2026.04.03 and falls back to SHA256SUMS when <artifact>-checksums.txt is absent.
close_reason: Published stable v2026.04.02 artifacts verified (core/install/checksum/bridge); publish rerun fail-closed on pre-existing release tag as designed

title: Harmonize rr-install.sh version format with AGENTS.md and 0.1.0 product version
description: The  script currently enforces a CalVer format (YYYY.MM.DD or YYYY.MM.DD-rc.N) which conflicts with the project's stated  initial release version. This bead should update the  script to either: 1. Accept and correctly handle  as a valid version format, or 2. Clarify how  maps to a CalVer release (e.g., if  corresponds to ). The goal is to allow the installation of the  product release using the installer script.
close_reason: Accepted semver and CalVer in rr-install.sh normalize_version; added semver install case in scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh; validated with bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh (pass).

title: Define & Implement Semantic Asset Packaging for Hybrid Search
description: Investigate and define the packaging, installation, and verification mechanism for the local embedding assets required by the hybrid search. Implement the necessary Rust code to load and utilize these assets within roger-storage's prior_review_lookup.
close_reason: Completed via child rr-tcx.1: semantic asset manifest install/load/verify implemented in roger-storage and consumed by prior_review_lookup for truthful lexical-only degradation when assets are absent or mismatched. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr <temp_suite_dir>

title: Implement semantic asset manifest install and verification hooks for hybrid search
description: Scope slice for rr-tcx: define a local semantic asset manifest shape, persist/load under Roger sidecar storage, verify artifact presence and digest, and expose a storage-level verification result consumable by prior-review lookup callers. Acceptance: (1) storage API can write/read semantic asset manifest deterministically; (2) verification fails closed on missing artifact or digest mismatch and reports structured reasons; (3) prior-review lookup callers can consume verification status to drive truthful lexical-only degraded reporting without claiming semantic parity. Validation contract: cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke; cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke; cargo test -p roger-storage
notes: Validation actually run: 1) rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-storage 2) rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke 3) rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke 4) rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage 5) tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); cp tests/suites/int_search_semantic_asset_packaging.toml "$tmpdir/"; cp tests/suites/int_search_prior_review_lookup.toml "$tmpdir/"; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr "$tmpdir"
close_reason: Completed semantic asset manifest install/read/verify hooks with fail-closed missing-path and digest-mismatch behavior, wired lookup degraded-mode gating to manifest verification, and added suite metadata. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo fmt -p roger-storage; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test semantic_asset_packaging_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage --test prior_review_lookup_smoke; rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-storage; tmpdir=/var/folders/d7/lj1cjbfs2gjb0gff7n541pf00000gn/T/tmp.ueo3eYgOdY; cp tests/suites/int_search_semantic_asset_packaging.toml "/"; cp tests/suites/int_search_prior_review_lookup.toml "/"; rch exec -- cargo run -p roger-validation -- plan pr ""

title: Implement Caching for Git Information Retrieval
description: Implement a simple caching mechanism for git commands (e.g., infer_repository_from_git, infer_git_branch) within roger-cli to improve CLI responsiveness. This is a first step towards optimizing git interactions.
notes:
Validation 2026-04-01:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli cached_per_repo_path
Result: pass (tests::repository_lookup_is_cached_per_repo_path + tests::branch_lookup_is_cached_per_repo_path).

Outcome:
- Existing git lookup cache implementation is present in packages/cli/src/lib.rs and now validates cleanly; prior compile blocker note is no longer applicable in current workspace state.
close_reason: Revalidated cache contract: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli cached_per_repo_path (2 cache tests passed).

title: Add supported-browser smoke coverage for PR-page launch on Chrome Brave and Edge
description:
The plan and release matrix treat Chrome, Brave, and especially Edge as first-class supported browsers, but the current bead graph does not visibly track a browser-smoke bead for the PR-page launch workflow. This should not automatically become a second heavyweight E2E; it should start as targeted supported-browser smoke around the serious bridge path and truthful launch-only fallback.

Acceptance:
- add supported-browser smoke coverage for PR-page Roger launch wiring on Chrome, Brave, and Edge
- verify Native Messaging happy path where available and truthful custom-URL/launch-only fallback where appropriate
- capture failure artifacts/transcripts per the validation harness contract
- keep coverage within the one-blessed-E2E budget unless the E2E budget is explicitly revised
notes: Graph clarification 2026-04-07: rr-v6x is the implementation lane for executable supported-browser smoke coverage. The separate usage-proof scenario bead is rr-0qvg.6, which should not close this parent by prose or metadata alone.
close_reason:
Closed parent implementation lane after landing both required child slices:
- rr-v6x.1 (already closed): lower-layer bridge launch-path integration coverage for native-messaging preference, launch-only fallback, and artifact expectation helpers.
- rr-v6x.2 (closed in this cycle): executable per-browser smoke runners behind existing suite ids via packages/bridge/tests/smoke_browser_launch_chrome.rs, smoke_browser_launch_brave.rs, smoke_browser_launch_edge.rs, with shared assertions in packages/bridge/tests/support/browser_smoke.rs.

Acceptance proof:
1) Supported-browser smoke coverage now has real executable runner entrypoints for Chrome/Brave/Edge, not metadata only.
2) Native Messaging and truthful custom-url launch-only fallback behavior are exercised by runner assertions using choose_launch_path + handle_bridge_intent.
3) Transcript/envelope capture and failure-artifact contract is asserted through required_launch_artifacts plus roger-validation failure_artifact_paths for each suite id.
4) E2E budget guard remains within one blessed heavyweight E2E (observed_heavyweight_e2e_count=1).

Validation commands executed in this slice:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-bridge --test bridge_smoke --test smoke_browser_launch_chrome --test smoke_browser_launch_brave --test smoke_browser_launch_edge
- rch exec -- cargo run -q -p roger-validation --bin roger-validation -- plan release tests/suites target/test-artifacts | rg 'smoke_browser_launch_(chrome|brave|edge)'
- rch exec -- cargo run -q -p roger-validation --bin roger-validation -- failure-paths smoke_browser_launch_chrome smoke_browser_launch_brave smoke_browser_launch_edge --metadata-dir tests/suites --artifact-root target/test-artifacts
- rch exec -- cargo run -q -p roger-validation --bin roger-validation -- guard-e2e-budget tests/suites docs/AUTOMATED_E2E_BUDGET.json

title: Add lower-layer launch-path integration coverage for supported-browser smoke prerequisites
description: Before browser-specific smoke claims can be trusted, the shared launch path needs explicit lower-layer coverage for Native Messaging and launch-only/custom-URL fallback decisions, envelope handling, and artifact capture expectations that the browser smoke runners will rely on.
acceptance_criteria:
Integration coverage exercises the shared launch path for Native Messaging happy path and truthful launch-only/custom-URL fallback without relying on docs or browser-manual inference
Failure-artifact and transcript expectations used by supported-browser smoke are asserted in code or harness helpers
Closeout runs the relevant bridge/integration suite commands and records the actual results

title: Fix cross-process artifact-id collisions in rr review continuation state
description:
Manual continuation-path rehearsal uncovered a deterministic cross-process ID collision in the CLI review path: repeated process invocations can generate duplicate artifact IDs (`bundle-<now_ts>-1`) and fail with `failed to persist ResumeBundle: sqlite error: UNIQUE constraint failed: artifacts.id`.

Repro commands (same repo + RR_STORE_ROOT, robot mode):
1) `rr review --pr 42 --robot` (exit 0)
2) `rr review --pr 43 --robot` (exit 0)
3) `rr review --pr 99 --provider codex --robot` (expected degraded Tier A path) -> observed exit 1 error due duplicate artifact ID.

Acceptance:
1. CLI-generated IDs used for session/run/bundle/binding are unique across separate `rr` process invocations sharing one store.
2. The codex bounded-provider review path returns the documented degraded/blocked semantics instead of internal storage errors in the above repro sequence.
3. Add lower-layer regression coverage that executes the CLI as separate processes (not only in-process `run()` calls) and asserts no duplicate-artifact-ID failure for shared-store continuation/review paths.
4. Record validation commands and outcomes in close reason.

Validation contract: integration layer via `cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke <new_process_invocation_test>` plus manual smoke replay of the repro sequence above.
notes: Blocked for now: required files packages/cli/src/lib.rs and packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs are under active exclusive reservations by PearlGorge (expires around 2026-04-07T06:46Z). Re-opened to avoid unsafe overlap; retry claim when reservations clear or coordinate handoff.
close_reason: Fixed cross-process artifact-id collision coverage by adding separate-process regression execution fallback in packages/cli/tests/session_aware_cli_smoke.rs. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-cli --test session_aware_cli_smoke (pass, includes separate_process_review_sequence_avoids_cross_process_artifact_id_collisions). Manual smoke repro with shared RR_STORE_ROOT: cargo run -q -p roger-cli --bin rr -- review --repo owner/repo --pr 42 --provider opencode --robot (exit 0), --pr 43 --provider opencode --robot (exit 0), --pr 99 --provider codex --robot (exit 5 degraded bounded support warning, no UNIQUE constraint).

title: Implement CalVer GitHub Actions release system for Roger 0.1.x
description: Turn the already-approved release/install contracts into a real GitHub Actions release system for Roger. This epic exists because rr-001.2 and rr-001.3 closed as planning-definition beads: they clarified artifact classes, install/update expectations, and ownership, but they did not create or land implementation work for version derivation, matrix builds, checksum verification, publication, or release-channel automation. The repo currently has validation workflows only (.github/workflows/validation-*.yml), which means CI exists in a narrow testing sense but CD does not. This epic should deliver a truthful, GitHub-Actions-native CalVer release path that can build Roger core artifacts, package bridge/extension lanes separately, verify asset completeness and checksums, and publish tagged releases with explicit approval boundaries. Validation contract: close only after child beads land and a GitHub Actions rehearsal proves a full release candidate artifact set can be built and verified without ad hoc maintainer assembly.
notes: Reopened 2026-04-07 for graph truthfulness: child rr-xr6.5 was reopened because its recorded closeout evidence does not satisfy its own release-publish validation contract. Epic should not be considered closed again until release-publish proof is mapped explicitly.
close_reason: Epic acceptance revalidated: all implementation children are closed (`rr-xr6.1` through `rr-xr6.6`), and GitHub Actions rehearsal evidence proves end-to-end release automation without ad hoc local asset assembly. Evidence set: release-build-core success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905681, release-package-bridge success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905678, release-verify-assets success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877984703, and release-publish success https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195. The release-publish-plan artifact for run 23878015195 records upstream run provenance, generated notes/checksums/manifests, and truthful support posture (`core_plus_bridge` with extension explicitly narrowed), satisfying the explicit publication-gate and lane-truth contract. CI-sensitive closeout evidence guard passed via scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-xr6 --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success.

title: Define Roger CalVer tags, channels, and version-derivation contract
description: Freeze the concrete CalVer scheme Roger will use for GitHub releases and artifact names, including stable vs rc/pre-release channels, tag format, rerun/build metadata rules, Cargo workspace version interaction, and how GitHub Actions derives the canonical release version without hand edits. This bead exists because the old release beads stopped at artifact ownership and install semantics; they never answered the operational versioning question, so there is no machine-truthful source for tags, release names, archive names, or self-update lookup. Acceptance: (1) one canonical CalVer format is documented and implemented in a small Roger-owned derivation path; (2) stable/rc/nightly semantics and provenance rules are explicit; (3) artifact names and release metadata fields derive from the same source; (4) the contract states why prior rr-001.2/rr-001.3 closure was insufficient: ownership was defined, but version authority was not. Validation contract: int layer via a deterministic version-derivation test command and a CI dry-run on synthetic refs.
close_reason: Implemented CalVer contract + derivation path in roger-validation; added docs/CALVER_RELEASE_VERSIONING_CONTRACT.md and .github/workflows/release-calver-dry-run.yml; validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-validation --test calver_derivation && rch exec -- cargo test -p roger-validation && synthetic dry-run refs via cargo run -q -p roger-validation -- derive-calver (stable/rc/nightly matrix checks)

title: Add release-build-core GitHub Actions matrix for Roger companion archives
description: Implement the release-build-core lane the canonical plan and release matrix already call for. The workflow should build versioned Roger core companion archives for the blessed target matrix, stamp them with the derived CalVer version, and emit reproducible archive names plus machine-readable metadata for downstream verification and publication. This bead exists because the repo currently validates tests on ubuntu only but does not build any publishable companion archives; the old beads defined ownership but never created the actual matrix-build job. Acceptance: (1) GitHub Actions builds the required core targets or a truthful first shipped subset with explicit narrowing; (2) each archive includes rr and minimal runtime metadata; (3) filenames are versioned and stable; (4) the job exports structured outputs consumed by downstream verification/publish jobs. Validation contract: int layer via workflow/action smoke plus a release-candidate run that produces the expected archive manifest on GitHub Actions.
notes: Validation evidence (2026-04-01): gh workflow run release-build-core.yml --ref main -> runs 23821379428 and 23821455643 reproduced unsupported macOS runner lane (macos-13-us-default) and blocked aggregate-core-manifest. Applied matrix fix in .github/workflows/release-build-core.yml (x86_64-apple-darwin lane now runs on macos-14), pushed isolated validation branch brightpuma/rr-xr6-2-macos-runner-fix (commit fb0e99b), then ran gh workflow run release-build-core.yml --ref brightpuma/rr-xr6-2-macos-runner-fix -> run 23821543593 SUCCESS (derive-release-metadata + 4 build-core lanes + aggregate-core-manifest). Downloaded artifact with gh run download 23821543593 -n release-build-core-manifest; manifest release-core-manifest-2026.03.31-nightly.fb0e99bbe223.json reports schema roger.release-build-core.v1, built_target_count=4, targets={aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin,x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu}, excluded_targets={aarch64-pc-windows-msvc}, matrix_scope=first_shipped_subset.
close_reason: Implemented and validated release-build-core matrix lane with truthful first-shipped subset + explicit exclusion metadata; confirmed GitHub Actions aggregate manifest generation via successful run 23821543593 on validation branch after fixing unsupported macOS runner mapping.

title: Add release-package-bridge and release-package-extension lanes with truthful optionality
description: Implement separate packaging lanes for Native Messaging registration bundles and extension sideload packages, preserving the plan requirement that browser artifacts stay optional and never get implied by the core release lane. This bead must wire contract verification for generated bridge bindings before extension packaging and must publish explicit lane metadata so release notes can say core-only vs core-plus-bridge vs core-plus-bridge-plus-extension truthfully. This bead exists because rr-007.1 froze the contract but did not create the CI/CD implementation. Acceptance: (1) bridge bundles are packaged per supported OS claim; (2) extension package generation runs only after contract-drift verification; (3) release metadata records which optional lanes were actually built; (4) a skipped lane narrows support claims rather than silently pretending parity. Validation contract: int layer via workflow smoke plus existing bridge and extension smoke tests on packaged outputs.
notes:
Implemented optional release lanes and lane-truth metadata:
- Added workflows: .github/workflows/release-package-bridge.yml, .github/workflows/release-package-extension.yml
- Added scripts: scripts/release/package_bridge_bundle.sh, scripts/release/package_extension_bundle.sh, scripts/release/build_optional_lane_summary.py
- Added validation scripts: scripts/release/test_build_optional_lane_summary.sh, scripts/release/test_package_bridge_bundle.sh

Acceptance mapping:
1) Bridge bundles per OS claim: package_bridge_bundle + matrix workflow for linux/macos/windows.
2) Extension packaging after contract verification: release-package-extension has verify-bridge-contract job as explicit upstream dependency; package_extension_bundle also runs rr bridge verify-contracts before pack.
3) Optional-lane metadata: build_optional_lane_summary emits release optional-lane summary artifact with per-lane built/skipped state and artifacts.
4) Skipped-lane narrowing: summary computes support posture (core_only/core_plus_bridge/core_plus_bridge_plus_extension) and narrowed_claims.

title: Add release-verify-assets gate for checksums, manifests, and artifact completeness
description: Implement the release-verify-assets lane that recomputes SHA256SUMS, validates archive contents, checks release manifest completeness, and fails closed when any shipped asset or support claim is missing. This bead corrects the current gap where validation-release runs tests but there is no artifact-verification gate between build and publication. Acceptance: (1) every candidate release produces a SHA256SUMS file and machine-readable asset manifest; (2) verification detects missing archives, mismatched checksums, and lane-claim drift; (3) release jobs cannot publish when verification fails; (4) release notes or signing placeholders surface unsigned targets honestly instead of hiding them. Validation contract: int layer via fixture-backed asset-manifest tests plus a GitHub Actions rehearsal that intentionally exercises at least one failing verification case and one passing case.
notes:
Retro-audit evidence addendum (2026-04-07): remote run-level proof captured for the CI-sensitive verification gate.
- release-verify-assets workflow run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877984703 (status=completed, conclusion=success, event=workflow_dispatch).
- The run artifact `release-verify-assets-report/release-asset-manifest.json` shows schema `roger.release-verify-assets.v1`, `publish_gate.publish_allowed=true`, and optional-lane summary entries present.
- Downstream publication rehearsal consumed this verify output successfully: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 (release-publish, success), proving publish gating is enforced from verified artifacts rather than fixture-only local checks.
close_reason: Implemented release-verify-assets lane with deterministic verifier + fixture rehearsals. Added scripts/release/verify_release_assets.py to validate core archive presence/checksum/content shape, optional-lane artifact availability, and support-claim drift; emits publish gate metadata plus SHA256SUMS + release-asset-manifest.json + release-notes-signing.md (unsigned targets surfaced explicitly). Added scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh covering passing fixture and intentional missing-archive, checksum-mismatch, and lane-claim-drift failures. Added .github/workflows/release-verify-assets.yml with explicit pass rehearsal (fixture-rehearsal + verify-candidate-assets) and intentional-fail rehearsal (rehearsal-intentional-fail), plus publish-gate-sentinel that only opens when verification reports publish_allowed=true. Validation: python3 -m py_compile scripts/release/verify_release_assets.py; bash -n scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh; bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh; ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-verify-assets.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'.

title: Add approval-gated GitHub release publication workflow for CalVer tags
description: Implement the release-publish lane as an explicit approval-gated GitHub Actions workflow that turns verified artifacts into draft or published GitHub releases without ad hoc local maintainer assembly. The workflow should support release-candidate rehearsal, stable publication from approved CalVer tags, and truthful notes that state which artifact lanes shipped. This bead exists because the current repo has manual validation-release only, which is not a publish system. Acceptance: (1) publication runs only from verified upstream artifacts and approved refs; (2) draft versus stable release behavior is explicit; (3) release notes include support-lane truthfulness and checksum references; (4) publication cannot bypass the verification gate. Validation contract: int/manual-release layer via GitHub Actions dry-run or draft-release rehearsal on a non-production tag plus documented operator smoke.
notes: Reopened 2026-04-07 after closeout audit: prior close reason ("fk close path repaired via pinned br binary after localfix regression isolation") does not map acceptance criteria to actual validation and does not record the required GitHub Actions dry-run/draft-release rehearsal evidence. Re-close only with explicit upstream run URL/id, outcome, publish mode, and operator-smoke evidence per rr-aip.2 and docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md.
close_reason: Acceptance mapped and revalidated for rr-xr6.5: (1) publication is gated to verified upstream artifacts + approved refs via release-publish validate_upstream_run/reverify steps and publish_release.py approved-ref/publish_gate checks; exercised by successful release-publish run https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 (workflow_dispatch, success) with upstream run provenance recorded in release-publish-plan artifact. (2) draft vs stable behavior is explicit via workflow_dispatch publish_mode (draft|publish) and publish_release.py mode policy; validated by scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh pass cases for RC draft + stable publish requiring operator_smoke_ack. (3) generated notes include support-lane truth + checksum references + run provenance; validated by scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh assertions and release-publish-plan artifact from run 23878015195 (Support Claims + Checksums lines present). (4) verification bypass is fail-closed through schema/publish_gate/run-identity/run-url/optional-lane parity checks; validated by scripts/release/test_release_publish_workflow.sh, scripts/release/test_validate_upstream_run.sh, and fail-case matrix in scripts/release/test_publish_release.sh. CI-sensitive evidence guard passed via scripts/swarm/check_ci_closeout_evidence.sh --bead rr-xr6.5 --run-url https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 --outcome success.

title: Implement Roger-owned installer metadata and self-update hooks against published CalVer releases
description: Implement the minimum Roger-owned release metadata consumption needed for the one-line install and update contract to become real: generated release metadata bundle shape, install-script lookup assumptions, and rr self-update plumbing against published CalVer releases. This bead exists because rr-001.3 defined install and update behavior, but there is still no shipped metadata or CLI plumbing to consume it. Acceptance: (1) release metadata bundle schema is finalized and generated by CI; (2) installer lookup rules for OS/arch/channel are aligned with publication outputs; (3) rr self-update or an equivalent internal update path can resolve and validate published metadata while failing closed on unpublished or local builds; (4) docs clearly state what is implemented now versus deferred. Validation contract: int layer via metadata-fixture tests and one end-to-end release-metadata rehearsal against a draft release or a local release-fixture mirror.
notes:
Retro-audit evidence addendum (2026-04-07): CI/release proof tied to published metadata path.
- Core metadata-producing run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23877905681 (`release-build-core`, completed/success) generated `core-install-metadata` consumed by publish lanes.
- Publish run: https://github.com/cdilga/roger-reviewer/actions/runs/23878015195 (`release-publish`, completed/success) attached verified assets for tag `v2026.04.03` and recorded upstream provenance in `release-publish-plan`.
- Live installer-readiness smoke (from rr-kz4 close evidence): at 2026-04-02T02:15:52Z, `releases/latest` resolved HTTP 200 to `v2026.04.03` and `bash scripts/release/rr-install.sh --repo cdilga/roger-reviewer --dry-run` exited 0 against published metadata.
This supplements the prior local fixture tests with explicit remote release evidence.
close_reason: Completed install-metadata contract closeout: documented rr self-update deferment + installer-driven published-update path in docs/RELEASE_AND_TEST_MATRIX.md; validated via bash scripts/release/test_build_install_metadata_bundle.sh && bash scripts/release/test_rr_install.sh && bash scripts/release/test_verify_release_assets.sh

title: Add session-aware CLI smoke and degraded-capability coverage
description: Non-overlapping rr-018 support slice focused on smoke/degraded acceptance coverage for CLI review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh.
acceptance_criteria:
Smoke scenarios cover review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh from repo-local CLI entry with blessed OpenCode assumptions
Coverage includes stale locator handling that falls back to ResumeBundle or explicit recovery guidance without silent session retargeting
Coverage includes degraded or unsupported provider capability outputs and verifies they are explicit and truthful
Artifacts remain in tests/docs/fixtures lanes and do not duplicate rr-018 command-path implementation ownership
notes: Coordination note: created because br ready is empty and rr-018 is the active frontier. This bead is intentionally non-overlapping support for rr-018 and should not modify primary command routing implementation.
close_reason: Added CLI smoke/degraded coverage plus fixture metadata and passing test run
